filters.predicate: l_shipdate >= 8766 AND l_shipdate < 9131 AND l_discount >= 5 AND l_discount <= 7 AND l_quantity < 2400

scan.access_pattern: sequential predicate passes with selective late materialization

indexes.purpose: skip_blocks fast-path only; if all blocks survive, bypass blockwise path and run contiguous row-range scan
skip_blocks fast-path only; do not build per-block metadata when overlap ratio is 100%
skip_blocks fast-path only; use only for candidate-density check before choosing scan kernel

optimization_notes: Root cause: Category B algorithm change. The current 100000-row zone maps prune 0 of 600 blocks, so the hot path degenerates into a branch-heavy full scan with sparse random probes into cache-exceeding 480 MB discount and quantity columns; that access pattern is latency-bound and wastes the 88 MB LLC. Replace the block-oriented kernel with a contiguous row-range plan: first stream l_shipdate once and write a 59986052-bit qualifying bitmap, then stream l_discount and l_quantity sequentially to clear non-qualifying bits while compacting surviving row ids plus discount values into thread-local buffers, and finally late-materialize l_extendedprice only for the final survivors and reduce with thread-local sums. Keep the existing zone maps only as a cheap precheck and bypass them entirely when candidate density is 100%.

correctness_anchors.revenue_formula: SUM(l_extendedprice * l_discount) with scaled_i64 arithmetic; divide by 10000.0 for CSV output
